user_query:
I'm prepping a quick compliance review of our civic microsite before we roll out a media briefing in the Niger Delta. Please assess whether our tracking setup respects consent for non‑essential cookies and produce a few artifacts I can share with the team.

Use only these local inputs:
- input/privacy_policy.html
- input/site_config.json
- input/tracking.js

Deliverables:
1) Extract the cookies table from input/privacy_policy.html into a CSV at out/cookies.csv with header columns exactly: cookie_name,provider,purpose,expiry,type. Include one row per cookie listed in the policy table.
2) Inspect input/site_config.json and input/tracking.js and write a summary JSON to out/config_summary.json that includes:
   - consent_mode (from config),
   - block_non_essential_before_consent (from config),
   - for each of google_analytics, facebook_pixel, hotjar: enabled, load_on_first_page (from config), and a derived boolean loads_before_consent inferred from the tracking.js logic assuming the default state before the user takes any action.
3) Write a compliance report to out/compliance_report.md with three short sections: Findings, Discrepancies, Recommendations. In Findings, include a clear line: "Non‑essential trackers load before consent: Yes/No" and list which tools, if any, do so. In Discrepancies, list any mismatch between cookies/tools disclosed in input/privacy_policy.html and what is enabled in input/site_config.json (e.g., cookies disclosed for a tool that is disabled, or a tool enabled without a corresponding cookie disclosed). In Recommendations, outline concrete fixes (e.g., switch to opt‑in, block non‑essential scripts until consent, update policy to match configuration). Base all statements on the provided files; do not use the internet.
4) Draft a concise email to the campaign manager saved at out/email_to_manager.txt that summarizes the key risks and the proposed next steps in plain language, referencing the attached files by name. Keep the tone professional and community‑oriented (I’m a political analyst from the Niger Delta and want to emphasize trust and transparency).

Make sure your results are consistent across the outputs (e.g., the tools you flag in the report match the derived loads_before_consent values in out/config_summary.json), and that every claim can be traced to a line or setting in the provided files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
